steps.prompt: Which word is the main clause verb?

hints.imperio: 'imperio' is a noun in the ablative meaning 'in accordance with my command/authority'
hints.auctoritate: 'auctoritate' is a noun in the ablative meaning 'in accordance with authority'

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'sunt' — 'sint' is subjunctive after 'cum' (causal)
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'essent' — 'sint' is present
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'fuerint' — 'sint' is present

reveal.gloss: I will provide for / take care of

hints.Present indicative: Present would be 'provideo' — 'providebo' with -ebo ending is future
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'provideam' — 'providebo' is future indicative
hints.Future perfect: Future perfect would be 'providero' — 'providebo' is simple future

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see) — the base verb
hints.praevideo, praevidere, praevidi, praevis: These are the parts of 'praevidere' (to foresee) — a different compound
hints.invideo, invidere, invidi, invisum: These are the parts of 'invidere' (to envy) — a different compound

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'imperium' — 'imperio' is ablative of accordance
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'imperio' (same form), but with 'pro' it is ablative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'imperium' — 'pro' governs the ablative

hints.1st: 1st declension ablative ends in -a — 'imperium, -ii' is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension ablative ends in -e — 'imperium, -ii' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension ablative ends in -u — 'imperium, -ii' is 2nd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'auctoritas' — 'auctoritate' with -e ending is ablative

steps.prompt: What does 'Quae cum ita sint' transition to?
steps.target: A concluding promise — 'since these things are so, I will act'
choices: An introduction of a new topic
A concluding promise — 'since these things are so, I will act'

hints.An introduction of a new topic: 'quae cum ita sint' summarizes what came before and introduces a conclusion
hints.A question to the audience: This is a declarative conclusion, not a question
hints.A prayer to the gods: Cicero is making a political promise, not a religious invocation

translation: Since these things are so, I will take care of it in accordance with my authority and command.
